title: lippia longispicata
intro: Lippia longispicata, also known as African tea or bushveld tea, is a shrub native to southern Africa, characterized by its small white flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Lippia longispicata include the treatment of fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of Lippia longispicata include infusions, decoctions, and infusions made from its leaves and stems, which can be consumed as a tea or applied topically to the skin.
